customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high evaluation mode. Sarah is a comparison shopper who has been through multiple solar pitches ('I've done a lot of these') and is approaching this appointment as a structured evaluation, not an exploratory conversation. She is cooperative but not emotionally invested — the closer needs to create investment, not assume it.
unresolved_concerns: Credit qualification is unresolved: Sarah said 'No' to the 670 threshold question; her subsequent 'Oh, yes. I do. I'm sorry' most plausibly answers the program/cash question, not a credit correction. Closer should verify credit before investing in the full proposal.

Competitive context unknown: Sarah has seen other solar designs ('I've done a lot of these') but Bucky never asked which companies or what she has already been shown. Closer is walking in blind on competitive positioning.
Lease term and package structure: Sarah asked about lease terms and package differences at 02:25. Bucky deferred to the specialist. This is the first question the closer should expect.
sensitive_topics_raised: Child at home during appointment window: Sarah was interrupted by a child at 03:47 during a 10:00 AM appointment. Closer should confirm at the top of the call that Sarah has a clear window and plan for potential interruptions.
exact_promises_rep_made: Text reminder will be sent before the call
Specialist ('he') will call Sarah — closer should be aware Sarah expects a male voice or should clarify if different
closer_should_know: Credit qualification is ambiguous — Sarah said 'No' to the 670 threshold and the correction was never explicitly confirmed. Verify credit before the full proposal. Beyond that: Sarah is an experienced comparison shopper in evaluation mode; she will be comparing Ion against designs she has already seen. The closer's first job is to find out what she has seen and create a reason to stay on the call past the first five minutes.
